title: cassebeera inframarginalis
intro: Cassebeera inframarginalis, also known as C. fruticosa, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Celastraceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing protection against various diseases. Cassebeera inframarginalis can be prepared as a herbal decoction or powder for consumption, or used as a topical application for skin issues.
